title: What are prediction markets, the new form of sports betting promoted by the football World Cup?
subhead: Online platforms known as prediction markets, a form of largely unregulated online betting, have seen rapid growth, fueled in particular by the surging popularity of sports betting.

title: Iran-US talks to continue through the night in Switzerland
'Twisted Face,' the French Gestapo collaborator who arrested historian and resistance fighter Marc Bloch
Trump-backed candidate wins razor-tight Colombia presidential election
subhead: Abelardo de la Espriella, a 47-year-old lawyer who has never held public office, narrowly won Colombia's presidential runoff. De la Espriella, who had received Donald Trump's 'complete and total endorsement,' promises a return to power for Colombia's right-wing faction.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'France's top priority will be to regain control of its public and welfare finances'
subhead: Before stepping down, the governor of the Bank of France, in a letter to President Emmanuel Macron, listed France's shortcomings. Economist Jean Pisani-Ferry welcomes this approach in his column.

title: Mysterious whale 'necropolis' discovered at the bottom of the Indian Ocean
subhead: A team of researchers has uncovered hundreds of fossilized whale carcasses off the southwest coast of Australia. Spanning 1,200 kilometers, this is the largest, deepest and oldest cetacean bone graveyard ever observed.

title: Paris's Quai Branly Museum turns 20: A look back at its turbulent history
subhead: The Paris institution is celebrating its anniversary with great fanfare this weekend. But behind the festivities, the museum continues to face many challenges, particularly economic.

subhead: Hundreds of foreign fighters held in captivity in Ukraine find themselves in legal limbo, as Moscow refuses to include them in prisoner exchanges.
byline: Emmanuel Grynszpan,  Jedrzej Nowicki  and  Thomas d’Istria

title: After US signals another review of its military presence in Europe, allies grapple with Washington's ambiguities
subhead: On June 18, the US defense secretary announced a 'review' of the distribution of American NATO forces on the continent. The decision has raised concerns, even though it is being presented as a simple 'adjustment,' a senior US defense official told Le Monde.

title: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhead: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.
